steps: The given equation is $\frac{r}{38} = s + t$.
Multiply both sides by 38 to isolate $r$: $r = 38(s + t)$.

steps.title: Use the source rationale
steps.content: Choice C is correct. Multiplying both sides of the given equation by $38$ yields $r=38(s+t)$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice A is incorrect. This equation is equivalent to $r-38=s+t$, not $\frac{r}{38}=s+t$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice B is incorrect. This equation is equivalent to $r+38=s+t$, not $\frac{r}{38}=s+t$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice D is incorrect. This equation is equivalent to $38r=s+t$, not $\frac{r}{38}=s+t$.

choiceEliminations:
• Choice A — '$r = s + t + 38$': Adding 38 does not undo division by 38. ✗
  • Choice B — '$r = s + t - 38$': Subtracting 38 does not undo division by 38. ✗
  • Choice C — '$r = 38(s + t)$': Multiplying both sides by 38 correctly isolates $r$. ✓
  • Choice D — '$r = \frac{s+t}{38}$': This divides by 38 a second time rather than multiplying; gives the wrong result. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
